rulings.Case Title: DARRYL LEUNG VS. TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Expert Witness Information
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 1 - DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU'S Motion To Augment Expert Witness Information. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Moving party defendant Tu has not satisfied the requirements of CCP sec. 2034.620. The need for the proposed new experts should have been anticipated by Tu and the new experts disclosed, at the latest, in a supplemental disclosure. The Court also notes there appears to be almost no effort to meet and confer made by Tu before filing this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. While the Court would be disinclined to award sanctions on this motion in any event, the plaintiff's request is not clearly made and is unsubstantiated by calculation of a dollar amount in a declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 21, 2015, Line 2 - PLAINTIFF ELLEN PAO'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to quash (MTQ) is granted in part and denied in part. As to both subpoenas, the Court has balanced plaintiff's right to privacy against defendant's stated reasons for needing the discovery. As to the document categories in defendant's subpoena of reddit: 1. The parties have reached a stipulation. 2. Performance evaluations: MTQ granted- not directly relevant to present action 3. Complaints made by Ellen Pao: MTQ granted - overbroad, not directly relevant. 4.Complaints by other employees against Pao: MTQ granted - such complaints could involve almost anything, and are hence not directly relevant. 5. Documents discussing KBCP: MTQ denied in part. The subpoena may only seek documents referring to the present Pao v. KBCP lawsuit. 6. Pao's promotion to interim CEO: MTQ denied in part. The subpoena may only seek documents referring to Pao's compensation. 7. Other documents regarding Pao's promotion to CEO: MTQ granted. 8. The parties have reached a stipulation. 9. The anonymous letter: MTQ granted- request is too vague The Court finds no privacy waiver by plaintiff for not objecting to the prior subpoena of reddit, and notes that privacy waivers are not lightly found. See, e.g., Heda v. Sup.Ct. (1990) 225 CA3d 525, 530. As to the subpoena of Pao's former employer, BEA: The MTQ is granted in its entirety. The Court finds as a general matter that the discovery sought is too remote in time. In addition, as to each document category: 1. All personnel documents- overbroad, not directly relevant to present action 2. Compensation- not directly relevant 3. Performance evaluations- not directly relevant 4. Complaints made by Pao- overbroad, not directly relevant 5. Complaints against Pao- overbroad, not directly relevant 6. Any written agreements - overbroad, not directly relevant Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
